mnemonics.title: Radio Frequency Bands
mnemonics.content: VLF (Very Low): 3-30 kHz | LF (Low): 30-300 kHz | MF (Medium): 300-3000 kHz | HF (High): 3-30 MHz | VHF (Very High): 30-300 MHz | UHF (Ultra High): 300-3000 MHz | SHF (Super High): 3-30 GHz | EHF (Extremely High): 30-300 GHz. Mnemonic: Very Low Lynx My Hair Is Very Ugly, Some Ugly Ears Hear.

mnemonics.title: Wireless Propagation Types
mnemonics.content: GRound S-k-y: Ground wave = Low freq, follows ground | Sky wave = High freq, reflects off ionosphere | Line-of-sight = Microwaves, direct path only.

mnemonics.title: Infrared vs Radio Waves
mnemonics.content: Infrared: I Am Restricted (Inaccessible) - Can't pass through walls, Line-of-sight needed. Radio: I Can Reach Anywhere - Penetrates walls, longer range.

acronyms.usage: Used in Bluetooth for frequency switching to avoid interference
Propagation method requiring direct path between transmitter and receiver
Frequency band (like 2.4 GHz) reserved for unlicensed wireless devices
Satellite-based navigation system providing location and time
Network using Wi-Fi (IEEE 802.11) for local wireless connectivity
Spread spectrum technique used in Wi-Fi 802.11b

visualTips.visualization: Draw a ladder from low frequency (radio waves) to high frequency (gamma rays). Place radio waves at bottom (lowest frequency), microwaves above, then infrared, visible light, UV, X-rays, and gamma rays at top. Annotate key wireless technologies at their frequency positions.
Draw three parallel lines: ground wave curving along Earth surface, sky wave bouncing between Earth and ionosphere (cloud layer), and line-of-sight as straight horizontal line between two towers.
Draw Earth in center with three satellite rings: GEO (far, one complete circle), MEO (middle), LEO (near, many satellites). Show signal beams as cones from satellites to Earth.

analogies.analogy: Like water waves spreading along the shore - the wave follows the contour of the land (Earth's surface).
Like a mirror in the sky - radio waves bounce off the ionosphere (the 'mirror' in the atmosphere) back to Earth.
Like two people shouting across a clear field - they must see each other directly without any obstacle blocking the path.
Infrared is like a flashlight beam - it only works where it directly shines. Radio waves are like sound - they can go around corners and through walls.
Like a giant mirror in the sky - signals sent up to the satellite are reflected back down to reach distant locations on Earth.
